mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate above 8.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to reduced corporate earnings visibility and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) indicates strong supply-side momentum, which drives corporate profitability and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of INDPRO and GDPC1 fails to explain more than 15% of SPX return variance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening monetary cycle characterized by rising FEDFUNDS and elevated CPIAUCSL creates inflationary pressure, leading to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns in high FEDFUNDS/CPIAUCSL regimes show no significant deviation from baseline (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in CPIAUCSL signals disinflationary pressures that reduce central bank intervention urgency, creating a positive environment for equity risk-taking and generating S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns in disinflationary periods show no significant positive drift (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in housing starts (HOUST) above 600k signals strong consumer demand for durable goods, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns in high HOUST regimes show no significant positive drift (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and decline in unemployment (UNRATE) signals a tightening labor market, which boosts consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to explain more than 10% of SPX return variance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: High levels of consumer spending (RSAFS) combined with strong housing demand (HOUST) indicate robust economic activity, which creates inflationary pressures and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns in high RSAFS/HOUST regimes show no significant negative drift (t-test p > 0.10).
